meta.title: Crypto software development cost | Web3 scope & audits
meta.description: Crypto software development cost for MetaMask/WalletConnect, Solidity on Ethereum/Polygon/Base, audits, KYC/AML, custody, token standards (ERC-20/721/1155).

hero.h1: crypto software development cost: scope, risk, and delivery for Web3 products
hero.subtitle: MetaMask/WalletConnect, Solidity contracts, and EVM builds on Ethereum, Polygon, or Base with compliance-ready operations.

pain.title: When the scope is fuzzy, crypto gets expensive fast
bullets: Have you ever shipped a wallet connect flow and watched users drop off at the first signature prompt?
Have you ever delayed launch because you could not justify a Solidity smart contract audit to stakeholders?
Have you ever gotten stuck between KYC/AML compliance, custody decisions, and exchange integration with no clear plan?

benefits.title: A scope you can price
benefits.desc: You get a clear backlog, deliverables, assumptions, and boundaries so estimates map to real work, not guesses.

benefits.title: Security as a requirement
benefits.desc: Solidity smart contracts are built with tests, reviews, and an audit plan so you reduce avoidable risk before mainnet.

benefits.title: Compliance-ready operations
benefits.desc: KYC/AML flows, custody controls, and audit trails are defined early so you can operate with visibility and governance.

scope.title: What your Web3 build can include

useCases.title: Wallet-based sign-in dApp
useCases.desc: Connect with MetaMask or WalletConnect, handle signatures safely, and keep the UX clear for first-time users.

useCases.title: ERC-20 token utilities
useCases.desc: Mint/burn rules, permissions, and integrations aligned with how your token actually moves and is monitored.

useCases.title: NFT drops (ERC-721/1155)
useCases.desc: Collections, allowlists, mint flows, and contract events you can track for support and reporting.

useCases.title: Custody-backed operations
useCases.desc: Approval flows, limits, key management decisions, and logs that match your risk posture and compliance needs.

useCases.title: Exchange deposit/withdraw
useCases.desc: Exchange APIs plus on-chain analytics to reconcile deposits, withdrawals, and confirmations by address and event.

stack.title: A Web3 stack matched to your constraints

options.name: MetaMask
options.note: Wallet connect, signing, and permissions with clear prompts and safe message patterns.

options.name: WalletConnect
options.note: Multi-wallet support with strong mobile UX using QR and deep links where appropriate.

options.name: Solidity
options.note: EVM smart contracts with testing strategy, review checkpoints, and audit readiness.

options.name: EVM chains
options.note: Ethereum, Polygon, or Base selection based on fees, liquidity needs, and operational constraints.

options.name: KYC/AML
options.note: Compliance workflows mapped to your product flows, with traceability and data handling guidelines.

options.name: On-chain analytics
options.note: Event indexing, address monitoring, and dashboards so you can investigate issues quickly.

steps.title: Align on scope
steps.desc: You share your use case, chain preferences, and constraints; scope and assumptions are documented.

steps.title: Build and verify
steps.desc: Wallet flows, Solidity contracts, and integrations are implemented with tests and security reviews.

steps.title: Launch and operate
steps.desc: Deploy to the chosen EVM chain, set up monitoring, and runbook the compliance and custody workflows.

faq.q: What drives crypto software development cost the most?
faq.a: Scope and risk do. Smart contract complexity, ERC-20/721/1155 requirements, audit depth, KYC/AML needs, custody model, exchange integration, and the chain choice all change effort and timelines.

faq.q: Do I need a smart contract audit before launch?
faq.a: If contracts control value, permissions, mint/burn logic, or complex state, an audit is strongly recommended. The goal is to reduce avoidable failures and increase confidence before public usage.

faq.q: When does KYC/AML compliance become necessary?
faq.a: When your product touches fiat ramps, value transfers, regulated jurisdictions, or internal compliance policies. The right approach depends on your business model, users, and custody responsibilities.

faq.q: Is custody better than non-custodial for UX?
faq.a: It depends. Non-custodial keeps user control and can reduce your operational responsibility, while custody can streamline UX but increases security controls, monitoring, and compliance obligations.

faq.q: Can you integrate an exchange and reconcile deposits?
faq.a: Yes. Exchange APIs can provide transaction status, and on-chain analytics can validate confirmations and reconcile movements by address, token, and contract event.

ctaFinal.title: Get a clear estimate and a scope you can defend
ctaFinal.desc: Share your use case, target chain, token standard, and whether you need KYC/AML, custody, audits, or exchange integration.
